doc: Toy 3 of 3 (V1.3 'The Toys', ADR-0022 §3): a drag-to-strum harp. One big fader is the strum bar — stream /strum_bar [0..1] and the `strum` op plucks a note each time the position crosses a string boundary (both directions strum). Strings are scale degrees resolved through the tonal context, so an open full-diatonic scale reads as a harp glissando and stays in key. Change the key live with /context/root (MIDI) or reshape the scale with /context/s0../context/s6. Sweep /brightness [0..1] to open the plucked tone (a Good Button on filter cutoff + resonance). Set the string span with /strum/octaves [1..4].

nodes.doc: PUBLIC strum bar. Identity [0,1] pass-through; its position streams to the strum op. Send /strum_bar [0..1] (the fader). Rests at 0.0 (bottom string) so no note fires at load.
Position crossings -> degree plucks. 8 strings over 1 octave by default; /strum/octaves widens the span.

C major, 12-TET. The strum degrees resolve through this, so the harp is always in key; change /context/root to transpose, /context/s0..s6 to reshape the scale. The Key control steps the root across one octave (C3..C4).
Degree plucks -> per-Voice freq + gate. Polyphonic so overlapping plucks ring together (a glissando).
Triangle tone — a soft, harp-like pluck timbre.
Cutoff + resonance are Signal inputs, driven by the Brightness Good Button.
Percussive pluck: near-instant attack, short decay, no sustain — each pluck rings out on its own. Gated by the voicer; the strum op's paired note-off frees the voice.
Shape the linear envelope CV into an exponential-style volume curve (x^2).
PUBLIC Good Button. Identity [0,1]; fanned to the two ranged maps below. Send /brightness [0..1]. Rests at 0.6 so the plucks are bright at load.
Smooth the mapped cutoff into a CV signal; default 4000 Hz before any knob move.
Smooth the mapped resonance into a CV signal; default 0.3.
